What is a gigajoule?

A gigajoule (GJ) is a measure of energy. One GJ of natural gas can:

Heat enough water for 95 hot showers*

*Five minute shower with 9.5 litres per minute showerhead.

Reduce your usage: install a water-efficient showerhead to save hot water.

Keep you cosy by the fire 11 winter evenings*

*With a 28,500 BTU model for three hours a night.

Reduce your usage: choose a high-efficiency EnerChoice® fireplace when upgrading.

Heat your whole home for a day*

*With a 120,000 BTU furnace running eight hours in a 2,000 sf home.

Reduce your usage: set your thermostat no higher than 20 °C for when you want heat.

Barbecue a months’ worth of burgers*

*On a 32,000 BTU/hr gas barbecue, one hour a day for 30 days.

Reduce your usage: don’t open and close the lid too often as this will cause the barbecue to lose heat.

Sear, sauté or stir-fry three months’ worth of dinners*

*On a 20,000 BTU/hr natural gas cooktop burner for 30 minutes a day for 90 days.

Reduce your usage: keep pans and pots covered to reduce heat loss.


Need help with this page?

Why does the number of days in the billing cycle differ from one bill to the next?
Billing periods vary depending on the number of days and weekends in a month and public holidays.

Should I download my consumption history in TXT or CSV format?
Either file type will open in most spreadsheet and text editor programs, such as Microsoft Excel or Google Docs.

Can I view my consumption history without comparing it to the previous year?
Yes. To disable comparison data, uncheck the show last year as comparison checkbox located beneath the date range drop down menu, then click the update or download button.

What is a gigajoule? How is it measured?
A gigajoule (GJ) is a unit of measurement used to determine the amount of energy contained in natural gas. One GJ is roughly the amount of energy needed to heat a typical house for one day during cold winter weather. You pay for the gas you consume, measured in GJ.

Learn more about gigajoules.

How is the average temperature determined?
Temperature data for your region is averaged out over the total number of days in the billing period. This temperature gives you an idea of outdoor conditions relative to your energy consumption.

Can I view consumption history for multiple accounts on one graph?
No. You can only view one account at a time.

7 a.m. to 8 p.m.
